location.origin: Olympia and Seattle, Washington. LAKE is rooted in both cities, operating across the Olympia-Seattle axis. Squaxin Island/Nisqually ancestral territory (Olympia) and Duwamish ancestral territory (Seattle). K Records artists -- embedded in Olympia's Independent Pop Underground community.
location.formative: Olympia, Washington. The K Records ecosystem -- Calvin Johnson's community of lo-fi pop artists, the International Pop Underground, Beat Happening's legacy. Olympia as the philosophical center of independent pop music in the Pacific Northwest. The second Olympia label in the engine after Kill Rock Stars (degree 70).
location.career: LAKE: Oh, the Places We'll Go (2007, K Records), Let's Build a Roof (2009, K Records), Bull (2012, K Records), The World Is Real (2015, K Records). Jangly, lo-fi indie pop with dual male/female vocals (Eli Moore and Ashley Eriksson). K Records -- the heart of the International Pop Underground. Ashley Eriksson wrote 'Island Song' (Adventure Time closing credits). First K Records artist in the engine.

labels: K Records (Olympia, WA -- independent label founded 1982 by Calvin Johnson of Beat Happening; the most culturally significant independent label in Pacific Northwest history; heart of the International Pop Underground; first K Records artist in the engine; second Olympia label after Kill Rock Stars (degree 70); roster includes Beat Happening, The Microphones/Mount Eerie (Phil Elverum, degree 71), Beck (early work), Built to Spill (early work), Modest Mouse (early singles), Tiger Trap, The Blow, Mirah; anti-corporate philosophy -- music belongs to everyone; lo-fi aesthetics as political choice; the label that organized the International Pop Underground Convention (1991))

members.name: Eli Moore
members.note: Co-vocalist and co-writer in LAKE. Shares vocal duties equally with Ashley Eriksson -- genuine dual vocals, not lead-and-backup. Warm, conversational vocal delivery. The male half of LAKE's vocal dialogue.

members.name: Ashley Eriksson
members.note: Co-vocalist and co-writer in LAKE. Wrote 'Island Song' -- the closing credits song for Adventure Time (Cartoon Network, 2010-2018). K Records indie pop reaching millions through animation. Sweet, warm vocal delivery. The female half of LAKE's vocal dialogue.

associated_musicians.name: Horse Feathers
associated_musicians.role: Second Olympia label (degree 70, Kill Rock Stars)
associated_musicians.note: Both Olympia labels. KRS (Horse Feathers, 70) is the political/punk pillar -- riot grrrl, political independence. K Records (LAKE, 73) is the pop/communal pillar -- lo-fi sweetness, democratic music-making. Same city. Different philosophies. The two pillars of Olympia's music culture.

associated_musicians.name: Phil Elverum
associated_musicians.note: Elverum's The Microphones released on K Records. Same label, same values, different music. Elverum used K Records philosophy for atmospheric experimentation. LAKE uses it for jangly pop sweetness. The K Records ecosystem contains multitudes.

associated_musicians.name: Chong the Nomad
associated_musicians.note: Opposite approaches to pop. Chong's electronic pop is DAW-produced, beat-driven, studio-constructed. LAKE's indie pop is guitar-driven, lo-fi, played live in a room. Both pop. Different centuries of pop-making.

associated_musicians.name: Ivan & Alyosha
associated_musicians.note: Both share the jangle. Ivan & Alyosha's fraternal jangle (degree 56) is folk-pop jangle -- acoustic foundation, anthem-building. LAKE's jangle is indie pop -- lo-fi, K Records, communal warmth. Different positions on the jangle spectrum.

associated_musicians.name: Kimya Dawson
associated_musicians.note: Both share lo-fi, anti-establishment spirit. Dawson's anti-folk is philosophically aligned with K Records values. Both reject polish, both prize sincerity. Dawson's anti-folk is solo and confessional. LAKE's indie pop is communal and jangly.

theory_nodes.concept: The jangle (seventy-fourth acoustic element -- the bright, chiming, treble-rich guitar sound that defines indie pop; open chords, clean tone, rhythmic strumming creating a shimmering, sparkling quality; the K Records jangle -- warm, sweet, democratic, accessible; anyone can make this sound; first pure jangle element since the fraternal jangle at degree 56)
examples: LAKE (degree 73) -- jangly indie pop guitars. Open chords, treble-heavy tone, rhythmic strumming patterns creating a shimmering texture. The K Records jangle: warm, sweet, democratic. The sound of friends making music together.

Contrast with the layered loop (degree 72, Briana Marela) -- the layered loop is technology-mediated, one voice multiplied. The jangle is acoustic and immediate -- instruments played together in real time, the shimmer arising from guitar strings and rhythm.

Contrast with the fiddle line (degree 70, Horse Feathers) -- the fiddle line is a melodic thread, a single instrument weaving. The jangle is textural -- the overall shimmer of the guitar sound, a wash of bright harmonic color.

Connection to the fraternal jangle (degree 56, Ivan & Alyosha) -- both jangles. The fraternal jangle was specific to two guitarists in folk-pop. The K Records jangle is broader: the genre's defining texture.
The jangle carries the K Records philosophy: bright, warm, unpretentious, accessible. A cheap guitar through a clean amp produces a jangle. Democratic sound-making.
E=3 -- the jangle energy. Buoyant, warm, bouncing rather than driving. More energetic than E=2 whisper but less forceful than E=4 drive.
theory_nodes.description: The jangle is the seventy-fourth acoustic element in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes the bright, chiming, treble-rich guitar sound that defines indie pop -- open chords, clean tone, rhythmic strumming creating a shimmering quality. In LAKE's music, the jangle is the K Records jangle: warm, sweet, democratic, accessible. The sound of the International Pop Underground.

ancestors: The layered loop (degree 72, Briana Marela) -- technology-mediated texture. The jangle is acoustic and immediate.
The fraternal jangle (degree 56, Ivan & Alyosha) -- specific instance of the jangle. Degree 73's jangle is the genre-defining texture.
The fiddle line (degree 70, Horse Feathers) -- linear melodic thread. The jangle is spatial shimmer.
genealogy.relationship: Seventy-four acoustic elements across seventy-four degrees. The jangle follows the layered loop -- from technological multiplication to acoustic shimmer. Track element 75 at degree 74.

theory_nodes.concept: The K Records collective (seventy-fourth artist-city pattern -- the artist whose relationship to the city is mediated through a label community; K Records as philosophy, community, and way of life; the label as home rather than business relationship; the International Pop Underground as cultural identity)
examples: LAKE (degree 73) -- K Records artists. Their connection to Olympia is inseparable from K Records. To be a K Records artist is to participate in a philosophical project: the International Pop Underground.
Contrast with the corridor wanderer (degree 70, Horse Feathers) -- geographic mobility within the I-5 corridor. The K Records collective is rooted in one city, defined by community.
Contrast with the international student (degree 72, Briana Marela) -- global circulation, training abroad. The K Records collective never leaves; it deepens.
Contrast with the visiting artist (degree 68, Beam) -- institutional connection without geographic presence. The K Records collective has deep geographic presence, embedded in community.
K Records as community: not just a business entity but a network of shared values, spaces, equipment, and audiences. Labels as communities rather than corporations.
Phil Elverum (degree 71) also in the K Records orbit -- The Microphones on K Records. The K Records collective pattern illuminates degree 71 retroactively.
theory_nodes.description: The K Records collective is the seventy-fourth artist-city pattern in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes the artist whose relationship to the city is mediated through a label that functions as a community, a philosophy, and a way of life. LAKE's connection to Olympia is inseparable from K Records. The International Pop Underground as cultural identity.

ancestors: The international student (degree 72, Briana Marela) -- global circulation. The K Records collective is local rootedness.
The corridor wanderer (degree 70, Horse Feathers) -- geographic mobility. The K Records collective is cultural embeddedness.
The visiting artist (degree 68, Beam) -- institutional connection. The K Records collective is communal presence.
genealogy.relationship: Seventy-four artist-city patterns across seventy-four degrees. The K Records collective follows the international student -- from global circulation to local rootedness. Track pattern 75 at degree 74.

theory_nodes.concept: Blue note -- the sweet imperfection (seventy-third deployment; the blue note that appears in the slight roughness of lo-fi indie pop; vocal cracks, fret buzz, timing wobbles, tape hiss; imperfections as warmth and humanity; the K Records blue note; not errors but the source of the music's charm)
examples: LAKE (degree 73) -- lo-fi indie pop production. The slight roughness of unprofessional recording: vocal cracks, guitar buzzes, timing wobbles. These imperfections are the music's warmth.
Contrast with the echo chamber (degree 72, Briana Marela) -- temporal displacement between voice and loop, technology-created. The sweet imperfection is organic -- human beings playing in real time.
Contrast with the thread (degree 70, Horse Feathers) -- specific intervallic phenomenon. The sweet imperfection is the total aesthetic of lo-fi roughness.
Contrast with the open chord (degree 69, Orlowski) -- clean, sustained simplicity. The sweet imperfection is the same chord with a slight buzz, a warm imprecision.

The K Records blue note: Calvin Johnson built K Records on the principle that anyone can make music. The sweet imperfection is the audible proof -- humans making music, and the imperfections are what make it beautiful.
theory_nodes.description: The sweet imperfection is the seventy-third blue note deployment in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes the blue note that appears in the slight roughness of lo-fi indie pop -- vocal cracks, fret buzz, timing wobbles that make the music warm and human rather than polished and mechanical. The K Records blue note. Not errors but the source of the music's charm.

ancestors: Blue note -- the echo chamber (degree 72, Briana Marela): technology-created temporal shimmer. The sweet imperfection is organic human warmth.
Blue note -- the thread (degree 70, Horse Feathers): specific intervallic phenomenon. The sweet imperfection is total lo-fi aesthetic.
All seventy-two prior blue note deployments (degrees 0-72)
genealogy.relationship: Seventy-three deployments across seventy-four degrees. Track deployment 74 at degree 74.

theory_nodes.concept: Indie Pop at Genre Stage 55 -- first pure Indie Pop classification in the engine; jangly guitars, sweet melodies, lo-fi production, dual vocals; the K Records genre; pop as democratic form; a continuation of the genre departure begun at Art Pop (degree 72)
examples: LAKE (degree 73) -- Indie Pop. Genre Stage 55. First pure Indie Pop classification.

Genre progression: Art Pop (72, Marela) to Indie Pop (73, LAKE). Both pop-adjacent. Both depart from folk/indie folk quadrant. Different construction: art pop adds through technology, indie pop adds through warmth.
Indie Pop defined by: jangly guitars, melodic accessibility, lo-fi production values, dual vocals and communal spirit.
K Records connection: Indie Pop is the K Records genre -- the pop song as democratic form, accessible to everyone.
Previous jangle-adjacent entries (Ivan & Alyosha, 54) were folk-pop, not pure Indie Pop. Degree 73 establishes the genre as its own category.
theory_nodes.description: Indie Pop at degree 73 establishes Genre Stage 55 -- the first pure Indie Pop classification in the Seattle 360 engine. Jangly guitars, sweet melodies, lo-fi production, dual vocals. The K Records genre. A continuation of the genre departure from folk/indie folk that began at degree 72. Pop as democratic form.

ancestors: Art Pop at degree 72 (Marela) -- Genre Stage 54. Both pop-adjacent, different construction methods. Art Pop adds through technology; Indie Pop adds through warmth.
Genre Stage 54 advances to Stage 55.
All prior genre stages in the engine.
genealogy.relationship: Genre Stage 55 at degree 73. First pure Indie Pop. Two consecutive pop-adjacent genres (Art Pop 72, Indie Pop 73). Track genre at degree 74.

theory_nodes.concept: E=3 at degree 73 -- nineteenth E=3 deployment; distribution 3:24:19:17:10:2; E=3 extends lead over E=4 (19 vs 17); the jangle energy -- buoyant, warm, communal; three E=3 entries in four degrees (70, 72, 73)
examples: LAKE (degree 73) -- E=3. Jangly indie pop. Buoyant, warm, bouncing rather than driving. More energetic than E=2 whisper but less forceful than E=4 drive.
E=3 rises to 19 deployments. Distribution after degree 73: E1:3, E2:24, E3:19, E4:17, E5:10, E6:2. Total: 75.
E=3 extends lead over E=4 (19 vs 17). E=2 remains dominant (24).
Three E=3 entries in four degrees: Horse Feathers (70), Briana Marela (72), LAKE (73). All create texture through layering.
The jangle energy: communal joy. Not aggressive (E=4+) and not quiet (E=2). The middle energy of friends making music.
theory_nodes.description: E=3 at degree 73 is the nineteenth E=3 deployment in the Seattle 360 engine. Energy distribution: E1:3, E2:24, E3:19, E4:17, E5:10, E6:2. E=3 extends its lead over E=4 (19 vs 17). The E=3 at degree 73 reflects the jangle energy -- buoyant, warm, communal. Three E=3 entries in four degrees.

ancestors: E=3 at degree 72 (Briana Marela): vocal looping energy. Dense layers. E=3 at 73 is jangly and bouncing.
E=3 at degree 70 (Horse Feathers): chamber ensemble. Three E=3 in four degrees.
E=3 lead grows: 19 vs 17 over E=4.
genealogy.relationship: Nineteen E=3 deployments across seventy-four degrees. Distribution 3:24:19:17:10:2. E=3 leads E=4. Track at degree 74.

theory_nodes.concept: K Records enters the engine at degree 73 -- Olympia-based independent label; founded 1982 by Calvin Johnson; heart of the International Pop Underground; second Olympia label after Kill Rock Stars (degree 70); arguably the most culturally significant independent label in Pacific Northwest history
examples: LAKE (degree 73) -- first K Records artist in the engine. K Records entering is a landmark event.
K Records founded 1982 by Calvin Johnson of Beat Happening. Olympia, Washington. The heart of the International Pop Underground.
Roster: Beat Happening, The Microphones (Elverum, degree 71), Beck (early), Built to Spill (early), Modest Mouse (early singles), Bikini Kill, Tiger Trap, Mirah, The Blow.
International Pop Underground Convention (1991) -- five-day festival in Olympia, one week before Nevermind. 'Love Rock Revolution Girl Style Now' opening night crystallized riot grrrl.
Second Olympia label after Kill Rock Stars (degree 70, Horse Feathers). KRS = political/punk pillar. K Records = pop/communal pillar. Two pillars of Olympia.
Phil Elverum (degree 71) in K Records orbit. The Microphones released on K Records. K Records at 73 retroactively illuminates degree 71.
theory_nodes.description: K Records enters the engine at degree 73 as an Olympia-based independent label founded in 1982 by Calvin Johnson. The heart of the International Pop Underground. Second Olympia label after Kill Rock Stars (degree 70). Arguably the most culturally significant independent label in Pacific Northwest history. The label that proved music belongs to everyone.

ancestors: Kill Rock Stars at degree 70 (Horse Feathers): political/punk Olympia label. K Records is the pop/communal Olympia label.
Phil Elverum at degree 71: K Records orbit. The Microphones on K Records.
Jagjaguwar at degree 72 (Marela): national indie. K Records is the local community label.
genealogy.relationship: K Records enters at degree 73. First K Records artist. Second Olympia label. Both Olympia pillars now in the engine. Track label at degree 74.

theory_nodes.concept: Dual vocals -- Eli Moore and Ashley Eriksson as genuine co-vocalists; the conversational pop song; male and female voices trading lines, harmonizing, creating dialogue; Ashley Eriksson wrote 'Island Song' (Adventure Time closing credits)
examples: LAKE (degree 73) -- Eli Moore and Ashley Eriksson sharing vocal duties as genuine co-vocalists. Trading lines, harmonizing, answering each other.
Distinct from communal harmony (degree 53, Fleet Foxes): many voices blending into one. Dual vocals preserve individual identity -- two people, not a choir.
Distinct from layered loop (degree 72, Briana Marela): one voice multiplied by technology. Dual vocals are two different people singing together -- no technology needed.
Ashley Eriksson wrote 'Island Song' (Adventure Time closing credits). K Records indie pop reaching millions through animation.
The conversational pop song: dialogue rather than monologue. The pop song as conversation between two voices.
theory_nodes.description: Dual vocals at degree 73: Eli Moore and Ashley Eriksson as genuine co-vocalists in LAKE. The conversational pop song -- male and female voices trading lines, harmonizing, creating dialogue. Ashley Eriksson wrote 'Island Song' (Adventure Time closing credits) -- K Records indie pop reaching millions through animation.

ancestors: Communal harmony (degree 53, Fleet Foxes): many voices blending. Dual vocals preserve individual identity.
Layered loop (degree 72, Briana Marela): one voice multiplied. Dual vocals are two different people.
The fraternal jangle (degree 56, Ivan & Alyosha): jangly interplay. Dual vocals add the vocal dimension.
genealogy.relationship: Dual vocals at degree 73. First dual male/female co-vocalist structure. Track vocal structure at degree 74.

theory_nodes.concept: Territorial acknowledgment: Squaxin Island/Nisqually and Duwamish territory at degree 73 -- Olympia-Seattle axis; dual territorial acknowledgment; seven territories mapped
examples: Degree 73 -- Squaxin Island/Nisqually territory (Olympia) and Duwamish territory (Seattle). LAKE operates across the Olympia-Seattle axis.
K Records rooted in Olympia -- Squaxin Island and Nisqually ancestral lands.

Seven Coast Salish territories remain mapped: Duwamish (Seattle), Multnomah (Portland, 57), Lummi/Nooksack (Bellingham, 58), Snohomish (Everett, 63), Puyallup (Tacoma, 66), Squaxin Island/Nisqually (Olympia, 70).
theory_nodes.description: The territorial acknowledgment at degree 73 recognizes Squaxin Island/Nisqually territory (Olympia) and Duwamish territory (Seattle). LAKE operates across the Olympia-Seattle axis. K Records is rooted in Olympia. Seven Coast Salish territories remain mapped.

ancestors: Squaxin Island/Nisqually territory mapped at degree 70 (Horse Feathers).
Duwamish territory at multiple prior Seattle degrees.
Seven territories mapped through degree 73.
genealogy.relationship: Dual territorial acknowledgment at degree 73. Seven territories mapped. Track at degree 74.

cross_references.relationship: Second Olympia label. KRS (Horse Feathers, 70) and K Records (LAKE, 73) -- the two pillars of Olympia's music culture. KRS is the political/punk pillar. K Records is the pop/communal pillar. Same city. Different philosophies. Both independent. Both in the engine.

K Records orbit. Elverum's The Microphones released on K Records. Same label, same values, different music. Elverum used K Records for atmospheric experimentation. LAKE uses K Records for jangly pop sweetness. The ecosystem contains multitudes.

Electronic pop contrast. Chong's electronic pop is DAW-produced, beat-driven. LAKE's indie pop is guitar-driven, lo-fi, played live. Both pop. Different centuries of pop-making. Chong builds in software. LAKE builds in the room.

Jangly positive. Both share the jangle. Ivan & Alyosha's fraternal jangle is folk-pop. LAKE's jangle is indie pop -- lo-fi, K Records, communal warmth. Different positions on the jangle spectrum. Both warm.

Anti-folk / K Records adjacent. Both reject polish, both prize sincerity over production. Dawson's anti-folk is solo and confessional. LAKE's indie pop is communal and jangly. Same philosophy, different expressions.
